author: Rahim Bakhsh, M.
title: Nur-i-Dil
gloss: A book containing three poems of which one is in the praise of Mirza Ghulam Ahmad of Qadian and two in the praise of God with one page in rose directed against the Christian Bible.

title: Shagl-i-Fakir
gloss: Two short poems,—one on the transitory character of the world and the other devotional.

printer_verbatim: Vijaya Bharat Press, Dera Ismail Khan.
author: Tilok Chand, Munshi
title: Guldasta-i-Mahrum

author: Wali Muhammad Khan
title: Munajat ba Dargah-i-Qazi-ul-Hajat almusamma ba Panah-i-Mahshar
gloss: A prayer in Urdu verse for salvation on the day of judgment.

author: Thakar Mukhtar Mahdi
title: Sorish-i-Hind par ek nazar
gloss: A pamphlet on the present agitation in India, in which the author counsels his countrymen to be loyal to the British Government.
notes: Only entry under URDU—POLITICS this quarter.

author: Abdul Wahid, Haji
title: Defence Islam Hissa Awwal
gloss: A booklet in which the author tries to prove that the story of Christ's crucifixion given in the scriptures relates to another man, named Shamun, whom people mistook for Christ.
notes: [Previous edition noticed at page __ in entry No. __ of the catalogue for the quarter ending the __ ] — cross-reference left blank by the clerk.

author: Abdul Wahid, Haji
title: Defence Islam, Hissa Awwal, Chaqchundar Hissa Awwal
gloss: Part I, of a series of tracts written in refutation of the Mirzai's belief regarding the crucifixion and resurrection of Christ and his reappearance.
notes: Author given as dash (continues serial 24's author).
